title: Callista trigonopus
intro: There is not enough information available about the Callista trigonopus plant.
taxonomy: Callista trigonopus is classified within the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, order Trochida, and family Calliostomatidae. This classification reflects its position among marine snails. The order Trochida specifically refers to top-shaped or disk-shaped snails, which aligns with the shape of Callista trigonopus. The family Calliostomatidae is a group of marine gastropod mollusks commonly known as top shells. These classifications help to understand the evolutionary relationships between Callista trigonopus and other related species.
uses: Callista trigonopus is known to have various medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Some studies suggest that Callista trigonopus may also have antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties.
